marketState: neutral with a mild bullish tilt — hot 8-16D weather and a rising fair-value curve offset by a large storage surplus and weak, low-reliability seasonality
summary: NG=F sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.875 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status across all horizons), so there is no valuation edge either way right now. The strongest signal in the data is weather: 8-16D demand is 20.5% above the 5-year norm and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.86), while the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.923 today to $3.025 by +21D, both supportive of higher prices into late summer. That is offset by a storage surplus of +180 Bcf vs the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and by seasonality that is weak and explicitly flagged low-reliability (only 38% odds of a higher price in 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range — an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk if the hot weather persists or storage surprises turn bullish. Technicals are split: intraday (5m/15m/1h) momentum is mildly bullish while the daily and weekly trend remain bearish (RSI ~36, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA), so no timeframe agreement on the short-term tape.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is 20.5% above 5y norms near the top of its range, the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.923 now to $3.025 by +21D, and managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of its 5y range — a crowded short vulnerable to a squeeze heading into seasonally strong September.
longTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.65 alongside continued bearish storage surprises (builds exceeding forecast) would confirm the supply overhang dominates and invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is essentially at fair value (FAIR status, front spread only -$0.048), so there is no near-term mispricing edge. Technicals actively disagree — 5m/15m momentum is bullish (RSI 58.6, MACD+, 50>200 EMA) while the daily/weekly trend is bearish (RSI ~36, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA) — and futuresCurve data is unavailable to confirm the term-structure skew.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a close above ~$3.00 with the 4h/1d EMA flipping 50>200 (confirms bullish momentum aligning with the hot weather), or a pullback to $2.65–2.70 for a better-risk long entry, or a confirmed breakdown below $2.65 to trade with the existing daily/weekly downtrend.
